title: Refusal for failure of original signature or proper certification; effect of recordation; necessity of proof of signature recordation of a duplicate
doc_html:
Art. 3344. Refusal for failure of original signature or proper certification; effect of recordation; necessity of proof of signature recordation of a duplicate

            A. Except as otherwise provided by law, the recorder shall refuse to record:

            (1) An instrument that does not bear the original signature of a party.

            (2) A judgment, administrative decree, or other act of a governmental agency that is not properly certified in a manner provided by law.

            B. Recordation does not dispense with the necessity of proving that the signatures are genuine unless they are authenticated in the manner provided by law.

            Acts 2005, No. 169, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 2006; Acts 2005, 1st Ex. Sess., No. 13, §1, eff. Nov. 29, 2005; Acts 2020, No. 254, §1.

NOTE: Acts 2005, 1st Ex. Sess., No. 13, §1, changed the effective date of Acts 2005, No. 169, to July 1, 2006.

recordation; necessity of proof of signature recordation of a duplicate
A.  Except as otherwise provided by law, the recorder shall refuse to record:
(1)  An instrument that does not bear the original signature of a party.

is not properly certified in a manner provided by law.
B.  Recordation does not dispense with the necessity of proving that the signatures
are genuine unless they are authenticated in the manner provided by law.
